bug_report.summary: Nested-placeholder snippet insertion leaves a literal “$2” token in the document text
steps_to_reproduce: Create a snippet with trigger `nest` and content: `Here is a ${1:nested ${2:placeholder}}.`
In a blank line in the editor, type `:` to open the snippet menu.
Filter/select `nest` and press Enter to insert.
expected_behaviors: Inserted text should be `Here is a nested placeholder.` (no raw placeholder tokens like `$1`/`$2` visible in the document).
actual_behaviors: Inserted text shows a literal `$2` in the editor (visible after `placeholder.`), indicating the placeholder token was not fully parsed/removed.

bug_report.summary: Creating a new Markdown file does not open it as the active tab in the main editor (new file ends up in lower-right pane / main editor remains on another document)
steps_to_reproduce: Click Save to create the new file.
Observe the main editor tab bar and content area (then open any top menu like Edit to confirm state persists).
expected_behaviors: The newly created file opens and becomes the active tab in the main editor; main editor content/word count reflects the new blank document.
Tab placement is consistent (new file tab appears in the primary editor tab bar, not a different pane).
actual_behaviors: After change, the main editor remains on the existing “Welcome to Zettlr!” document (non-empty content/word count), while the new file tab appears in the lower-right pane instead of the main editor tab bar (visible while Edit menu is open).

bug_report.summary: Opening Preferences unexpectedly changes the active document/content in the background editor (Helpful Links disappears; status bar resets to 0 words/0 characters)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the application to a state where a document (e.g., “Helpful Links”) is visible in an editor pane/split view.
Click the Settings/Preferences gear icon to open Preferences.
Observe the document content and word/character counts in the background panes behind the Preferences window.
expected_behaviors: Opening Preferences should not change which document/tab is active in any editor pane behind it.
Background content (e.g., “Helpful Links”) and status bar counts should remain unchanged while Preferences is open.
actual_behaviors: After change, opening Preferences causes the background lower-right pane to switch to an empty document (status bar shows 0 words / 0 characters) and the previously visible “Helpful Links” content is no longer shown (diff regions no. 4–9, 3, 5–7).

bug_report.summary: Typing ':' opens snippet menu in a different editor document/pane (keystroke goes to wrong document)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the app and navigate to a state with the lower-right pane showing an existing document (e.g., “Helpful Links”).
Click in the lower-right editor pane around the same location.
Type ':' to open the snippet selection menu.
expected_behaviors: The ':' keystroke should apply to the currently visible/active document in that pane (e.g., “Helpful Links”), and that document should become modified (asterisk on its tab).
The snippet menu should open anchored to the caret within that same document.
actual_behaviors: Before change: the snippet menu opens over the “Helpful Links” content and the tab indicates it is modified (e.g., “* Helpful Links”).

After change: the snippet menu opens but the underlying pane appears to be an empty document (status shows 0 words) and a different tab is marked modified instead (the ‘Helpful Links’ content is not present).
